differential_diagnoses: DRESS syndrome (Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms)
Kaposi's varicelliform eruption (superimposed on chronic dermatosis)
next_steps: Perform flow cytometry on peripheral blood to assess for Sézary cells
Excision of axillary/inguinal lymph node for histopathology and flow cytometry
Repeat skin biopsy with T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies and immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD7, CD30)
Bronchoscopy with biopsy of lung mass to rule out malignancy vs. granulomatous disease
Review medication list for potential culprit agents for drug eruption
Obtain infectious disease consultation for persistent purulent drainage and P. aeruginosa management
Monitor for metabolic complications of erythroderma (fluid/electrolyte balance, thermoregulation)

differential_diagnoses: Kaposi's varicelliform eruption (superimposed on chronic dermatitis)
next_steps: Excision of an enlarged lymph node for histopathology, immunohistochemistry, and flow cytometry
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for Sézary cell detection
Bronchoscopy with biopsy and T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies of the pulmonary mass
Repeat skin biopsy with T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies
Infectious disease consultation to manage Pseudomonas superinfection and monitor for HSV dissemination
Evaluation for systemic mastocytosis given extremely high IgE levels
